Examples

  • Gallery

image

The image mark draws a raster image — an SVG <image href> box — at each row's (x, y), sized w × h pixels. The src channel is the image URL or data: URI; (x, y) is the box's top-left corner (the same anchor convention as rect). Like every mark, positional channels take scale bindings, layout outputs, or pixel literals, and w/h are pixel sizes.

const birds = db.derive("birds", `SELECT *, 'data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10" fill="%234e79a7" fill-opacity="0.8"/></svg>' AS icon_url FROM penguins`);
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.image(birds, {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("body_mass_g"),
  w: 24, h: 24,
  src: field("icon_url"),          // reads the icon_url COLUMN (a bare string would be one literal URL)
}).axis("x").axis("y");
return v;

A scatter where each point is a 24×24 image loaded from its row's icon_url column, its top-left corner placed at the scaled (x, y).

Options

Positional x/y place the box's top-left corner. w/h are pixel sizes (default 0, so you must set them or nothing is drawn). src is the content channel — bind it to field("col") for per-row URLs, or pass a bare string for one constant image on every row (the bare-string-is-a-literal rule). opacity rides the box's style.

channel type default meaning
x positional-x — box top-left x (scale binding, layout output, or pixel literal)
y positional-y — box top-left y
w pixel 0 box width in px
h pixel 0 box height in px
src text "" image URL or data: URI. field("col") = column; bare string = literal
opacity number 1 image opacity

Examples

A single logo placed at a fixed pixel point. src is a bare string, so the SAME image is drawn once at (x, y) rather than per row.

const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.image("penguins", {
  x: 20, y: 20, w: 48, h: 48,
  src: "https://example.com/logo.png",
});
return v;

Per-row images from a column, sized uniformly. field("icon_url") reads each row's URL; w/h are pixel literals so every glyph is the same size.

const birds = db.derive("birds", `SELECT *, 'data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10" fill="%234e79a7" fill-opacity="0.8"/></svg>' AS icon_url FROM penguins`);
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.image(birds, {
  x: linear("bill_length_mm"),
  y: linear("body_mass_g"),
  w: 20, h: 20,
  src: field("icon_url"),
}).axis("x").axis("y");
return v;

Faded thumbnails via opacity, layered under a dot overlay so the points stay legible.

const birds = db.derive("birds", `SELECT *, 'data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10" fill="%234e79a7" fill-opacity="0.8"/></svg>' AS icon_url FROM penguins`);
const v = canvas(640, 460);
const x = linear("bill_length_mm"), y = linear("body_mass_g");
v.image(birds, { x, y, w: 32, h: 32, src: field("icon_url"), opacity: 0.35 });
v.dot("penguins",   { x, y, r: 2, fill: "#333" });
return v;

Images placed into a layout's solved boxes — borrow a bandX × barY box and drop a data-sized image into it. w/h come from the layout instead of literals.

const birds = db.derive("birds", `SELECT *, 'data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10" fill="%234e79a7" fill-opacity="0.8"/></svg>' AS icon_url FROM penguins`);
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.bar("penguins", {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.15 }).pick("x", "w"),
  ...barY("body_mass_g").pick("y", "h"),
  fill: "#eef",
});
v.image(birds, {
  ...bandX("species", { paddingInner: 0.15 }).pick("x", "w"),
  ...barY("body_mass_g").pick("y", "h"),
  src: field("icon_url"),
});
return v;

See also

  • text — the sibling glyph mark (a string box instead of a raster box)
  • rect — the same top-left (x, y) + w × h box convention, filled instead of imaged
  • bandX / barY — box layouts to place images into
